summary: Backend testing of injury preview, apply-injury-update, and extended profile fields. All 21 tests passed (100%).

minor.issue: The change_doc inserted into db.substitutions is missing the 'timestamp' field (not included in the raw dict), so the substitution log entry sorts to the bottom when GET /api/substitutions queries by timestamp descending. The SubstitutionLog model defines timestamp with default_factory but the endpoint bypasses it via raw dict insert.

action_items: MINOR: In apply-injury-update endpoint, add 'timestamp' field to change_doc dict so substitution logs sort correctly. Fix: add '"timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc)' to change_doc.
critical_code_review_comments: apply-injury-update inserts raw dict to db.substitutions without 'timestamp' field, breaking timestamp-based sort order in GET /api/substitutions
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created; existing profile used
context_for_next_testing_agent: All new endpoints (injury-preview, apply-injury-update, extended profile PUT/GET) are fully functional. Minor: apply-injury-update's substitution log entry lacks 'timestamp' field.
rca_of_issue: apply-injury-update builds change_doc as a raw Python dict (line 343-351) without including 'timestamp'. The SubstitutionLog model has timestamp=Field(default_factory=...) but the raw insert bypasses the model. Fix: add 'timestamp': datetime.now(timezone.utc) to change_doc, or use SubstitutionLog(**change_doc).to_mongo() pattern.
